note: As-is baseline (before any code change), verified against the real layout of architecture.mmd. Three edge-routing defects on a dense flowchart TB: (#1) four edges attach to RULES's top at correctly-spread ports but all jog at the SAME y=1190 -> one collinear knot that separateLanes (overlap gate) and separateAntiParallelJogs (pair-scoped, first-interior-run) both miss; (#2) MCP's lone in-top + lone out-bottom edges both at offset 0 (x=400) -> a straight vertical skewers MCP; (#3) the stream-context/findings verticals run through the ENGINE container box because subgraph boxes are post-layout and never fed into routing.

diagrams.title: As-is: convergence tangle at RULES top (4 jogs collinear at y=1190), MCP skewer at x=400, and ENGINE-container cut by the long BE<->RULES verticals
